title: Lance Comfort

infobox.value: (11 August 1908 – 25 August 1966) was an English [film director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director"). He was a prolific maker of [B movies](/wiki/B_movies "B movies") from 1945 to 1965.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English film director (1908–1966)

| Lance Comfort | |
| --- | --- |
| Directing [Jess Conrad](/wiki/Jess_Conrad "Jess Conrad") in *[Rag Doll](/wiki/Rag_Doll_(film) "Rag Doll (film)")* (1961) | |
| Born | (1908-08-11)11 August 1908  [Harrow](/wiki/Harrow,_London "Harrow, London"), [London](/wiki/London "London"), England |
| Died | 25 August 1966(1966-08-25) (aged 58)  [Worthing](/wiki/Worthing "Worthing"), [Sussex](/wiki/Sussex "Sussex"), England |
| Occupations | Film director, producer |
| Years active | 1945–1965 |

**Lance Comfort** (11 August 1908 – 25 August 1966) was an English [film director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director"). He was a prolific maker of [B movies](/wiki/B_movies "B movies") from 1945 to 1965.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lance_Comfort&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Lance Comfort was born in [Harrow, London](/wiki/Harrow,_London "Harrow, London")[[1]](#cite_note-so-1) on 11 August 1908.[[2]](#cite_note-bfi-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lance_Comfort&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

In a career spanning over 25 years, he became one of the most prolific film directors in Britain, though he never gained critical attention and remained on the fringes of the film industry, creating mostly [B movies](/wiki/B_movies "B movies").[[1]](#cite_note-so-1) He made a number of films for British National Pictures.[[3]](#cite_note-yule-3)

Comfort carried on working almost right up to his death in [Worthing](/wiki/Worthing "Worthing"), [Sussex](/wiki/Sussex "Sussex"), on 25 August 1966.[[2]](#cite_note-bfi-2)[[1]](#cite_note-so-1)

## Critical assessment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lance_Comfort&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Critical assessment")]

The film historians Steve Chibnall and [Brian McFarlane](/wiki/Brian_McFarlane_(writer) "Brian McFarlane (writer)") praise Comfort's gifts "in the confident exercise of melodramatic impulses in the interests of illuminating character and relationship, in a decorative visual style to serve these impulses, and in giving their heads to string of dominant actors". They add that all of his films "are persuasive narratives, marked by absence of sentimentality and the whiff of human reality".[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lance_Comfort&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Filmography")]

* *[Penn of Pennsylvania](/wiki/Penn_of_Pennsylvania "Penn of Pennsylvania")* (1941)
* *[Hatter's Castle](/wiki/Hatter%27s_Castle_(film) "Hatter's Castle (film)")* (1942)
* *[Those Kids from Town](/wiki/Those_Kids_from_Town "Those Kids from Town")* (1942)
* *[Squadron Leader X](/wiki/Squadron_Leader_X "Squadron Leader X")* (1943)
* *[Escape to Danger](/wiki/Escape_to_Danger "Escape to Danger")* (1943)
* *[When We Are Married](/wiki/When_We_Are_Married_(film) "When We Are Married (film)")* (1943)
* *[Old Mother Riley Detective](/wiki/Old_Mother_Riley_Detective "Old Mother Riley Detective")* (1943)
* *[Hotel Reserve](/wiki/Hotel_Reserve "Hotel Reserve")* (1944)
* *[Great Day](/wiki/Great_Day_(1945_film) "Great Day (1945 film)")* (1945)
* *[Bedelia](/wiki/Bedelia_(film) "Bedelia (film)")* (1946)
* *[Temptation Harbour](/wiki/Temptation_Harbour "Temptation Harbour")* (1947)
* *[Daughter of Darkness](/wiki/Daughter_of_Darkness_(1948_film) "Daughter of Darkness (1948 film)")* (1948)
* *[Silent Dust](/wiki/Silent_Dust "Silent Dust")* (1949)
* *[Portrait of Clare](/wiki/Portrait_of_Clare_(film) "Portrait of Clare (film)")* (1950)
* *[Douglas Fairbanks, Jr., Presents](/wiki/Douglas_Fairbanks,_Jr.,_Presents "Douglas Fairbanks, Jr., Presents")* (1953–1957)
* *[The Girl on the Pier](/wiki/The_Girl_on_the_Pier "The Girl on the Pier")* (1953)
* *[Bang! You're Dead](/wiki/Bang!_You%27re_Dead "Bang! You're Dead")* (1954)
* *[Eight O'Clock Walk](/wiki/Eight_O%27Clock_Walk "Eight O'Clock Walk")* (1954)
* *[The Man in the Road](/wiki/The_Man_in_the_Road "The Man in the Road")* (1956)
* *[Face in the Night](/wiki/Face_in_the_Night "Face in the Night")* (1957)
* *[Man from Tangier](/wiki/Man_from_Tangier "Man from Tangier")* (1957)
* *[At the Stroke of Nine](/wiki/At_the_Stroke_of_Nine "At the Stroke of Nine")* (1957)
* *[The Ugly Duckling](/wiki/The_Ugly_Duckling_(1959_film) "The Ugly Duckling (1959 film)")* (1959)
* *[Make Mine a Million](/wiki/Make_Mine_a_Million "Make Mine a Million")* (1959)
* *[The Breaking Point](/wiki/The_Breaking_Point_(1961_film) "The Breaking Point (1961 film)")* (1961)
* *[Rag Doll](/wiki/Rag_Doll_(film) "Rag Doll (film)")* (1961)
* *[Pit of Darkness](/wiki/Pit_of_Darkness "Pit of Darkness")* (1961)
* *[The Painted Smile](/wiki/The_Painted_Smile "The Painted Smile")* (1961)
* *[Touch of Death](/wiki/Touch_of_Death_(1961_film) "Touch of Death (1961 film)")* (1961)
* *[The Break](/wiki/The_Break_(1963_film) "The Break (1963 film)")* (1962)
* *[Tomorrow at Ten](/wiki/Tomorrow_at_Ten "Tomorrow at Ten")* (1962)
* *[Blind Corner](/wiki/Blind_Corner "Blind Corner")* (1963)
* *[Live It Up!](/wiki/Live_It_Up!_(film) "Live It Up! (film)")* (also known as *Sing and Swing* in the U.S.) (1963)
* *[Be My Guest](/wiki/Be_My_Guest_(film) "Be My Guest (film)")* (1965)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* *[Devils of Darkness](/wiki/Devils_of_Darkness "Devils of Darkness")* (1965)
